title: StateSnapshot
description: JSON projection of the `StateSnapshot` struct returned by the emergency module's forensic snapshot function (`peerx-contracts/counter/src/emergency.rs`). Soroban contracts exchange data as XDR `ScVal`, not JSON, so this schema describes the canonical JSON representation that off-chain forensic/indexing tools should produce when decoding a snapshot's XDR payload, e.g. via `stellar-xdr` or `soroban-client`. Rust tuples and `Vec<(K, V)>` maps are projected as arrays of named objects for readability; 128-bit and 64-bit integers are encoded as decimal strings because they can exceed JavaScript's safe integer range (2^53 - 1).

balances.description: Snapshot of per-user, per-asset balances. Corresponds to `Vec<((Address, Symbol), i128)>`.

pool_xlm.description: Total XLM liquidity held by the pool at snapshot time.

pool_usdc.description: Total USDC liquidity held by the pool at snapshot time.

total_fees.description: Total accumulated protocol fees at snapshot time.

badges.description: Snapshot of per-user badge grants. Corresponds to `Vec<((Address, Symbol), bool)>`.

tiers.description: Snapshot of per-user tier assignments. Corresponds to `Vec<(Address, Symbol)>`.

paused.description: Whether the contract's emergency pause switch was engaged at snapshot time.

frozen_users.description: Addresses frozen by the emergency admin at snapshot time. Corresponds to `Vec<Address>`.

block_volume.description: Per-block traded volume recorded by the circuit breaker. Corresponds to `Vec<(u64, i128)>`.

i128String.description: Signed 128-bit integer encoded as a base-10 string to avoid precision loss.

u64String.description: Unsigned 64-bit integer encoded as a base-10 string to avoid precision loss.
